title: How to recommend a treatment without offending the patient
filename: How to recommend a treatment without offending the patient.en.vtt
excerpt: So how do you recommend a treatment without causing offense to apply [Music] well this is really easy when you figure out what we're actually trying to do with aesthetic treatments is as i always say you're trying to change someone's story so you need to understand what their sto…

segments.text: so how do you recommend a treatment without causing offense to apply [Music] well this is really easy when you figure out what we're actually trying to do with aesthetic treatments is as i always say you're trying to change someone's story so you need to understand what their story is i don't think it's our job to judge patients almost in a paternalistic way and say this is wrong with your face you need to have it in order to be happy i always say to my patients you're the expert on happiness on your happiness and on yourself i'm the expert on facial aesthetics together we work as a team to create a treatment design that's going to maximize your happiness because ultimately that's the only goal i think we're striving for if you change a an aesthetic appearance on someone's face change the way they look and it doesn't make them happier arguably you've added no value i mean there are plenty of people who aren't 10 out of 10 who live happy fantastic lives and are never held back by their life by their looks there are people with scars on

their faces who for some reason can get over it and don't worry about it and don't let it hold them back and you could argue that the physical intervention the risk that goes with that unless you improve the psychological function that you've actually achieved very little at all and this is the key when you say without causing offense which is you will cause offense if you tell them something that is not part of their story and mostly what this really starts with is you need to get their story first what are they really trying to achieve are they trying to look more confident more beautiful younger fresher whatever it is and then if the thing you're spotting relates to something they've told you in terms of their goals they will never be offended because you're trying to help them achieve what they're there for so not offending your client starts by always structuring your treatment plan around the goal that they are trying to achieve it is never about what you think will look good on their face it's about what they need changing on their face in order to put them on the front foot in life and feel confident and go out there and make the most of their opportunity to live a full life

so as long as you're grounding your treatment plan in the reason that they're attending their real story which is never their treatment requests where people so often go wrong most patients will come in and ask for a specific treatment that isn't actually telling you what their story is once you discover their story you can recommend almost any treatment that will help them achieve their particular goal it's related to the conflict they're trying to overcome which is essentially what a story is so start with their needs advise them accordingly and you will never cause offence hope that helps you
